---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}

```

## TikTok content observation report

This dynamic report summarises the TikTok videos I observed over time.

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

logged_data <- read_csv("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vRzHPTFILyuMsB346F4vOapFEE6kB0BuvMZ2pH-dxTkeXFYXK0GrGbAwVbHYXIVoXqyUleiNAhgnSRf/pub?output=csv")

# Look at the data
glimpse(logged_data)
names(logged_data)

# -----------------------------------
# Part D: Rename variables
# IMPORTANT:
# Replace the column names inside ` ` with your exact form question names
# -----------------------------------

latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(
    type = 2,
    engagement = 3,
    watched_full = 4,
    ad = 5,
    time = 6
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    engagement = as.numeric(engagement)
  )

# Check cleaned data
glimpse(latest_data)

# -----------------------------------
# Explore summary values
# -----------------------------------

# Total number of observations
latest_data %>%
  summarise(total_observations = n())

# Mean engagement score
latest_data %>%
  summarise(mean_engagement = mean(engagement, na.rm = TRUE))

# Highest engagement score
latest_data %>%
  summarise(max_engagement = max(engagement, na.rm = TRUE))

# Lowest engagement score
latest_data %>%
  summarise(min_engagement = min(engagement, na.rm = TRUE))

# -----------------------------------
# Explore bar charts
# -----------------------------------

# Bar chart 1: content type
latest_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = type)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of content types",
    x = "Content type",
    y = "Number of videos"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Bar chart 2: watched fully
latest_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = watched_full)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Whether videos were watched fully",
    x = "Watched fully",
    y = "Count"
  )

# Bar chart 3: advertising
latest_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ad)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Whether videos included advertising or promotion",
    x = "Advertising or promotion",
    y = "Count"
  )

# Bar chart 4: time observed
latest_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = time)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Time of day when videos were observed",
    x = "Time of day",
    y = "Count"
  )

# -----------------------------------
# Code selected for dynamic report
# -----------------------------------

# Summary value 1
latest_data %>%
  summarise(total_observations = n())

# Summary value 2
latest_data %>%
  summarise(mean_engagement = mean(engagement, na.rm = TRUE))

# Final bar chart 1
latest_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = type)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of content types",
    x = "Content type",
    y = "Number of videos"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Final bar chart 2
latest_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ad)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Whether videos included advertising or promotion",
    x = "Advertising or promotion",
    y = "Count"
  )
```
